text: McDonald's is facing a significant consumer boycott across the Middle East and in Muslim-majority countries worldwide, after its Israeli franchise operator publicized a campaign offering free meals to Israeli soldiers. The backlash spread rapidly on social media, with calls to boycott the chain gaining millions of supporters. McDonald's shares fell 5% following the news.

title: McDonald's strike: Thousands of workers demand living wage
text: The McDonald's strike comes amid growing momentum for labor reform in the fast food industry, with several states having already raised their minimum wages. Organizers say they will continue to pressure the company until it commits to a $15 minimum wage nationwide. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

title: McDonald's Happy Meal gets a healthy makeover

title: McDonald's wins lawsuit against ex-CEO over sexual misconduct cover-up
text: McDonald's has won a lawsuit against its former chief executive Steve Easterbrook, who agreed to repay approximately $105 million in compensation after the company revealed he had hidden evidence of additional sexual relationships with employees. Easterbrook was fired in 2019 for having a consensual relationship with an employee, which violated company policy. McDonald's declined to comment beyond its official statement.

title: Golden Arches go dark in Russia as McDonald's joins corporate exodus
author: Nathan Martin
text: McDonald's exit from Russia after more than three decades has been described as the symbolic end of a post-Cold War era of globalization. The company opened its first Russian restaurant in Moscow in 1990, with thousands of customers queuing around the block in a scene that became an icon of the fall of communism. Analysts said the situation could have lasting implications for the brand.

text: McDonald's exit from Russia after more than three decades has been described as the symbolic end of a post-Cold War era of globalization. text: The E.coli outbreak linked to McDonald's Quarter Pounders has now expanded to affect customers in more than a dozen states, with the CDC urging consumers to avoid the product. McDonald's has set up a dedicated hotline for affected customers and has committed to covering medical costs for those sickened. McDonald's shares fell 7% following the news.

title: McDonald's claws back millions from fired CEO in landmark case

Easterbrook was fired in 2019 for having a consensual relationship with an employee, which violated company policy. McDonald's declined to comment beyond its official statement.

text: The Golden Arches have gone dark across Russia after McDonald's completed the sale of its Russian business to a local franchisee. The restaurants have reopened under a new name, Vkusno i tochka, meaning 'Tasty and that's it', in a surreal echo of the original chain's menu and d�cor. McDonald's shares rose 8% following the news.

title: McDonald's reports strong quarterly earnings driven by higher prices
author: Elizabeth Shields
text: McDonald's is accelerating its digital transformation, with the company reporting that digital sales now account for more than 40% of revenue in its top markets. The company's loyalty program has attracted tens of millions of members since its launch. McDonald's shares fell 8% following the news.

title: McDonald's sales fall in Muslim-majority countries amid Gaza war
author: Ariel Ross
text: Sales at McDonald's restaurants have dropped sharply in several Middle Eastern countries, with the company acknowledging the impact of consumer boycotts linked to the conflict in Gaza. The company issued a statement saying it was not involved in political conflicts and that all of its restaurants were locally owned and operated. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

title: McDonald's tries to distance itself from Israeli franchise controversy
text: McDonald's has moved to contain the damage from a viral social media campaign linking the brand to the conflict in Gaza. The company has stressed that its restaurants in the Middle East are independently owned and operated by local franchisees, and that it does not take political positions.

title: McDonald's wins lawsuit against ex-CEO over sexual misconduct cover-up
author: James Evans
text: McDonald's has won a lawsuit against its former chief executive Steve Easterbrook, who agreed to repay approximately $105 million in compensation after the company revealed he had hidden evidence of additional sexual relationships with employees. Easterbrook was fired in 2019 for having a consensual relationship with an employee, which violated company policy. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

title: Workers at McDonald's and other fast food chains plan coordinated strike
text: Thousands of McDonald's workers walked off the job across the United States on Thursday, joining the Fight for $15 movement's call for a $15 minimum wage and the right to unionize. Strikes were reported in dozens of cities including New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, and Detroit. McDonald's declined to comment beyond its official statement.

title: Corporate exodus: McDonald's latest to leave Russia over Ukraine
text: McDonald's announced it would close all of its approximately 850 restaurants in Russia following the country's invasion of Ukraine, becoming one of the most prominent Western companies to exit the Russian market. The company said it could no longer ignore the humanitarian crisis caused by the conflict. McDonald's shares rose 8% following the news.

text: McDonald's announced it would close all of its approximately 850 restaurants in Russia following the country's invasion of Ukraine, becoming one of the most prominent Western companies to exit the Russian market. The company said it could no longer ignore the humanitarian crisis caused by the conflict. McDonald's shares fell 8% following the news.

title: The McDonald's boycott: How social media turned consumers against the brand
text: The boycott of McDonald's, Starbucks, and other Western brands has accelerated in Muslim-majority countries following the outbreak of the conflict in Gaza. Analysts say the movement represents one of the most significant consumer boycotts of Western brands since the Iraq War, with lasting implications for the companies' market positions in the region. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

title: McDonald's tries to distance itself from Israeli franchise controversy
text: Sales at McDonald's restaurants have dropped sharply in several Middle Eastern countries, with the company acknowledging the impact of consumer boycotts linked to the conflict in Gaza. The company issued a statement saying it was not involved in political conflicts and that all of its restaurants were locally owned and operated. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

title: How McDonald's CEO scandal exposed governance failures at the fast food giant
author: Charlotte Lema�tre
text: McDonald's has won a lawsuit against its former chief executive Steve Easterbrook, who agreed to repay approximately $105 million in compensation after the company revealed he had hidden evidence of additional sexual relationships with employees. Easterbrook was fired in 2019 for having a consensual relationship with an employee, which violated company policy. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

text: McDonald's announced it would close all of its approximately 850 restaurants in Russia following the country's invasion of Ukraine, becoming one of the most prominent Western companies to exit the Russian market. The company said it could no longer ignore the humanitarian crisis caused by the conflict. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.

text: McDonald's has announced a new limited-time menu item designed to attract younger customers, part of a broader strategy to modernize its offerings and appeal to changing consumer tastes. title: CDC investigation points to McDonald's onions as source of E.coli outbreak
author: Madeleine Bouchet
text: The E.coli outbreak linked to McDonald's Quarter Pounders has now expanded to affect customers in more than a dozen states, with the CDC urging consumers to avoid the product. Analysts said the situation could have lasting implications for the brand.

text: McDonald's is facing a significant consumer boycott across the Middle East and in Muslim-majority countries worldwide, after its Israeli franchise operator publicized a campaign offering free meals to Israeli soldiers. The backlash spread rapidly on social media, with calls to boycott the chain gaining millions of supporters. McDonald's shares fell 6% following the news.

title: McDonald's claws back millions from fired CEO in landmark case
text: McDonald's board of directors faced intense scrutiny this week after documents emerged suggesting senior executives had been aware of multiple complaints about Steve Easterbrook before his termination. title: How the Gaza war triggered a McDonald's boycott in Muslim countries

The company issued a statement saying it was not involved in political conflicts and that all of its restaurants were locally owned and operated. The company operates more than 40,000 restaurants in over 100 countries.
